title: TOON vs JSON for LLMs: Token Efficiency, Retrieval Accuracy, and Where It Actually Helps
author: Adam Holter
fetch_notes: Fetched cleanly via WebFetch. Critique focuses on TOON's narrow scope and structural weaknesses, framing it as 'a compression codec for prompts, not a new foundation for data engineering.'

related_sources.title: TOON vs JSON arxiv benchmark (constrained decoding)

content_summary: Balanced critique of TOON. Acknowledges 30-60% token savings on flat data and accuracy wins on retrieval, but flags wide-row 'header decay,' thin ecosystem, weak null handling, limited benchmark depth, and the model training-data gap. Concludes TOON is a prompt-layer codec, not a data-engineering substrate for JSON.
